Module.Title: Mass Heart for Daggerheart
Module.Description: Mass Effect-inspired science-fiction compendium module for the Foundryborne Daggerheart system.

0KQtWDyzf1V3cY6r.Description:
# Vanguard
Domains: Blade, Sage
#### Warlord
The Warlord is a powerful melee combatant that overpowers and locks down their enemies.
Domain: Valor
#### Slayer
The Slayer moves quickly around the battlefield, exchanging their defenses for damage in high-risk, high-reward gameplay.
Domain: Arcana
0KQtWDyzf1V3cY6r.Name: Vanguard

4us66OI0EVPuMKur.Description:
# Engineer
Domains: Codex, Arcana
#### Mechanic
The Mechanic works in tandem with their drone, using it as an extension of themselves.
Domain: Splendor
#### Saboteur
The Saboteur uses their enemies and the environment to tip the scales in their favor.
Domain: Midnight
##
4us66OI0EVPuMKur.Name: Engineer

8TSjVrk5lWRMarUZ.Description:
# Adept
Domains: Sage, Splendor
#### Nemesis
The Nemesis is a master of destruction, pouring maximum effort into violent displays of biotics.
Domain: Arcana
#### Bastion
The Bastion uses biotic interruptions to controls the battlefield and incapacitate your enemies.
Domain: Bone
::
##
8TSjVrk5lWRMarUZ.Name: Adept

AUKyTSxTwCdjtB10.Description:
# Soldier
Domains: Valor, Blade
#### Shock Trooper
The Shock Trooper is a mobile fighter that can use Adrenaline to push beyond their limits.
Domain: Bone
#### Commando
The Commando specializes in weapons, enhancing them with Munitions to be prepared for any situation.
Domain: Midnight
::
##
AUKyTSxTwCdjtB10.Name: Soldier

fR22ntEYpcVTExMJ.Description:
# Infiltrator
Domains: Midnight, Bone
#### Assassin
The Assassin employs lethal precision and capitalizes on Critical Successes.
Domain: Blade
#### Agent
The Agent expertly deceives opponents and security systems, sticking closely to the shadows.
Domain: Codex
::
##
fR22ntEYpcVTExMJ.Name: Infiltrator

zRXU7lCgHZNsj0vg.Description:
# Sentinel
Domains: Splendor, Codex
#### Guardian
The Guardian uses powerful shields to protect their team and enhance their abilities.
Domain: Valor
#### Raider
The Raider is a jack-of-all-trades that can catch people off-guard by weaving their many talents together.
Domain: Sage
::
##
zRXU7lCgHZNsj0vg.Name: Sentinel

0xZuJYUWfM2HrliW.Description:
Play the Slayer if you want to move quickly around the battlefield, risking your safety to deal more damage. Gain access to the Arcana domain.
Foundation Features Assault: Before you make an attack, you may mark a Shield Slot to use a d20 as your Hope die. If the attack succeeds against a target in Melee range, add 1d10 energy damage to the damage roll and knock them back to a Close range from you.
Battery: Gain Overshield 1 at the start of a scene. Additionally, you may gain Overshield 2 when you choose the Overcharge downtime move.
Specialization Features Heavy Assault: The energy damage from Assault increases to 2d10.
Living on the Edge: The first time each rest when all your Shield Slots are marked, clear 2 Stress. Additionally, when you would mark a Shield Slot but cannot, you can mark 2 Stress instead.
Mastery Features Unrelenting Assault: The energy damage from Assault increases to 3d10.
Nova: Once per rest, when you mark a Shield Slot from the Assault feature, you can mark an additional Shield Slot to use the attack roll against all targets in Very Close range.
0xZuJYUWfM2HrliW.Name: Slayer

49WUyk3j2iNvUnvo.Description:
Play the Guardian if you want to take hits for your team and support them. Gain access to the Valor domain.
Foundation Features Fortification: Once per long rest when you activate Tech Armor, gain Overshield equal to your Tier and these additional benefits until Tech Armor deactivates: resistance to physical damage, immunity to Restrained and Vulnerable, and you cannot be moved against your will.
Retaliate: When you mark a Shield Slot to reduce damage from an adversary in Close range while Tech Armor is active, they must mark a Stress.
Specialization Feature Protector: When an ally in Very Close range would take damage, you may mark a Shield Slot to reduce the damage by one threshold.
Defense Matrix: When Tech Armor ends, clear a Shield Slot.
Mastery Feature Tech Burst: Once per rest when Tech Armor ends, all adversaries in Very Close range must succeed on a Reaction Roll (17) or they are knocked back to Far range and become temporarily Vulnerable. Additionally, all allies in Very Close range clear one Shield Slot.
49WUyk3j2iNvUnvo.Name: Guardian

6SL8IE1YezAWFbT1.Description:
Play the Raider if you want to use your versatile skillset to keep your enemies guessing. Gain access to the Sage domain.
Foundation Feature Jack-of-all-trades: At the start of a session, roll an amount of d6 equal to your level. For each value, place an Affinity token on the corresponding trait from left to right on your character sheet. When you make a roll using that trait, expend Affinity to apply one distinct effect for each token spent: gain +1 to an action or reaction, add 1d6 to damage, or gain a Hope. Remove unused Affinity at the end of a session.
Specialization Features Flow State: When you make a Spellcast Roll with the bonus from Versatile Skillset, spend a Hope to use a trait with Affinity as your Spellcast Trait. Describe how you use this trait to cast this spell in a unique way.
Got your Back: When you Help an ally, spend one Affinity token from the trait that matches their action roll to give them the benefit.
Mastery Features Dreamteam: You are a rallying point for your allies. You can initiate a Tag Team Roll one additional time per session. Additionally, when you spend Affinity during a Tag Team that involves you, you may both choose an effect for each token spent.
6SL8IE1YezAWFbT1.Name: Raider

DflNE5ssMohraFeH.Description:
Play the Saboteur if you want to use the environment to tip the scales in your favor. Gain access to the Midnight domain.
Foundation Features Hack: Once per session, describe an unattended object in the area to hack for one benefit: give Overshield 1 to yourself and all allies; you and an ally clear a HP or a Stress; you gain 2 Hope or a handful of gold; or you learn one secret about the area.
Specialization Feature Sabotage: When an adversary in Close range attacks, make a Knowledge Reaction Roll against their attack roll. When you succeed, spend a Hope to have them mark a Stress or reroll their attack.
Hacker: Once per long rest, when an adversary fails an attack, you may apply one temporary condition: Jammed, Shocked, or Staggered.
Mastery Feature Hackst: You may use Hacker three times per long rest; each option once.
Demolitionist: Describe a technical item you wish to destroy within Far range, then make a Spellcast Roll. Once per long rest on a success, create a temporary hazardous area in Very Close range around the item. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Reaction Roll against the Spellcast result to escape. All creatures are Vulnerable while in the hazard.
DflNE5ssMohraFeH.Name: Saboteur

eHyJPnEbIP8SvrY9.Description:
Play the Warlord if you want to get in your enemies' face and lock them down. Gain access to the Valor domain.
Foundation Feature Opportunist: If an adversary within Melee range attempts to move, you can make a reaction roll using your primary weapon trait against their Difficulty. Choose one effect on a success, or two if you critically succeed: they cannot move, you deal your primary weapon damage to them, you move with them, or you gain a Hope or clear a Stress.
Specialization Feature Battlemaster: If an adversary within Melee range attempts to make an attack where you are not the target, you may also use Opportunist. You also gain extra options: become the target of their attack, or make them Vulnerable until they mark HP.
Lash: Once per rest, you can make a Spellcast Roll against an adversary in Far range. On a success, pull them into Melee range with you.
Mastery Features Lord of War: When you succeed on the reaction from your Opportunist Feature, you may choose one additional option. Additionally, once per rest, spend any amount of Hope to choose one more option for each Hope spent.
eHyJPnEbIP8SvrY9.Name: Warlord

eVGMeTENfWTepfIz.Description:
Play the Mechanic if you want to work in tandem with your drone as an extension of yourself. Gain access to the Splendor domain.
Foundation Features Zap!: When you damage a target in Melee range of your drone, add 1d6 to the damage roll.
Relay: You may mark a Stress to use your drone as the point of origin of a spell. Spells cast this way cannot trigger Zap!
Specialization Feature Drone Specialist: When you summon your drone, you can spend an additional Hope to give it a specialization: Defense gives adversaries in Melee range of the drone Disadvantage on attacks; Combat lets you make a Spellcast Roll against a target in Far range of your Drone to command an attack that deals d8+6 physical damage using your proficiency; Explosive makes all adversaries in Very Close range mark a Stress when the drone dissipates.
Mastery Feature Artificer: As an additional downtime move you can choose, create a simple consumable that expires on your next rest.
Delivery: When you summon your drone, you may attach one consumable from your inventory. Any ally in Melee range of your Drone can use the consumable, or you may command your drone to use it.
eVGMeTENfWTepfIz.Name: Mechanic

HY900I2m2JEO8Iti.Description:
Play the Assassin if you want to employ lethal precision to best your enemies. Gain access to the Blade domain.
Foundation Features Anticipation: After a rest, roll a d12 and place it on this card. When you roll your Duality Dice, you may mark 2 Stress to replace the value of your Fear die with the value of this die. If this results in a Success, remove it.
Headshot: When you critically succeed on an attack, duplicate a damage die of your choice.
Specialization Feature No Distractions: If you have not moved during your spotlight or there are no other characters in Close range of you, you may spend an amount of Hope up to your Tier to add to the result of your Hope Die.
High Expectations: Once per long rest, you may give the GM a Fear to roll a new die for your Anticipation feature.
Mastery Features Professional Standards: You may reroll any dice that land on a 1 until they land on a different value.
HY900I2m2JEO8Iti.Name: Assassin

J6LKdsWiImUQsHVI.Description:
Play the Agent if you want to deceive your opponents and strike at the most opportune moments. Gain access to the Codex domain.
Foundation Features Decoy: When you become Cloaked, create a stationary decoy that looks like you and is convincing from Close range and further. While the Decoy is active, you can spend a Hope to stay Cloaked even when you end your move in line of sight. The decoy deactivates when it takes damage, is out of range, or when you create a new Decoy.
I'm In: You gain Advantage on bypassing security systems and handling evidence, such as falsifying security logs, using cameras, finding or deleting footage, and setting off alarms.
Specialization Features Superposition: You may mark a Stress to swap your position with your Tactical Decoy, switching the target of any attacks. Additionally, you may immediately move a Close range when you become Cloaked.
Expertise: When you add an experience, you may mark a Stress instead of spending Hope to double the bonus.
Mastery Feature Pass without Trace: Once per rest, when you become Cloaked, you may spend any amount of Hope to make an additional ally in Close range also Cloaked for each Hope spent.
J6LKdsWiImUQsHVI.Name: Agent

KC6bsExTkH5ey9ig.Description:
Play the Bastion to use biotics to manipulate and control the battlefield. Gain access to the Bone domain.
Foundation Feature Cantrip: Mark a Stress to roll a number of d4 equal to your Instinct and place them on this card. Clear unspent dice at a long rest.
Biotic Trick: Expend a Cantrip die to hinder an adversary in Far range. Depending on its value: 1 subtracts 1d4 from a reaction roll; 2 rolls another damage die and discards the highest; 3 moves them a Very Close range horizontally; 4 lets you choose one option.
Specialization Features Barrier Siphon: When you succeed on a spell or deal energy damage against a target, spend cantrip dice to clear a Shield Slot for each multiple of 3 in their combined value.
Anchor: While you have no marked Shield Slots, you cannot be moved or lifted against your will. You may also use the result of 4 on a Cantrip die to temporarily Restrain an adversary instead.
Mastery Features Lift: When you Restrain an enemy, you can spend doubles from your Cantrip dice to make them Massless while they are Restrained. Massless characters float in place and are considered Vulnerable. If they are moved by any effect, they move one range increment further, ending Massless.
KC6bsExTkH5ey9ig.Name: Bastion

oqm14LK1994RIB5h.Description:
Play the Commando if you like to enhance your weapons with special Munitions to be prepared for anything. Gain access to the Midnight domain.
Foundation Features Bandolier: When picking this subclass, you gain access to two Munitions. When you reach a new tier, choose one more. Once per long rest, you may swap one Munition on your belt for another.
Locked & Loaded: Mark a Stress to load a Munition on a non-Heavy weapon until the end of the scene or until the GM ends it as a consequence. When you make a successful attack with that weapon, you can apply the effects of the Munition. Each weapon can only be loaded with one Munition at a time.
Specialization Features Special Reserves: When you use a consumable or spend Ammo from a Heavy Weapon, roll a d6. On a 6, it is not consumed.
Thriller: When you would gain Hope, you may choose to gain Adrenaline instead.
Mastery Feature Powerful Attacks: You can always spend 1 Adrenaline during a damage roll to roll another damage die and discard the lowest.
Munitions Inferno Rounds: The target must Mark a Stress. Cryo Rounds: The target has Disadvantage on their next reaction roll. Warp Rounds: The target's Damage Thresholds are reduced by your Tier until they clear a Hit Point. Disruptor Rounds: The target uses a d12 instead of a d20 for their next attack roll. Tungsten Rounds: Damage dealt cannot be reduced by any features. Shredder Rounds: If you deal Severe damage, the target marks an additional HP. Hammerhead Rounds: The target must succeed on a Reaction Roll (14) or they are knocked back a Close range. Phasing Rounds: Your attack roll ignores disadvantage on Hidden targets and may hit targets in cover. Radioactive Rounds: The next action against the target has Advantage. Chemical Rounds: The target becomes Corroded, reducing their difficulty by 1 until cleared.
oqm14LK1994RIB5h.Name: Commando

rhvLYidg0K8jtfhb.Description:
Play the Nemesis to exert yourself to become a master of biotic destruction. Gain access to the Arcana domain.
Foundation Features Warp: Make a Spellcast Roll against a target. On a success, spend a Hope to make the target Warped. Spellcast and damage rolls against a Warped target gain a bonus equal to your tier. This condition lasts until the GM spends Fear equal to your Instinct to remove it, until you Warp another creature, or until the scene ends.
Exert: Once per session, when you Fail with Fear on a Spellcast Roll from a Domain Card, you can place that card in your Vault to turn it into a Success with Fear. Its recall cost is increased by 2 until your next rest.
Specialization Features Unstable Warp: When a Spellcast Roll succeeds against your Warped target, you can end the condition and cause a Biotic Detonation. The target takes d20+3 additional energy damage using your proficiency.
Clear Mind: You may add the number of cards in your Vault to your energy damage rolls.
Mastery Feature Biotic God: Once per long rest, after you make a successful Spellcast Roll, you may cast an additional Spell using the same roll. Then, place that card in your Vault.
rhvLYidg0K8jtfhb.Name: Nemesis

RO6BoGdtB6amQG00.Description:
Play the Shock Trooper to be a mobile tank that can use Adrenaline to push beyond their limits. Gain access to the Bone domain.
Foundation Features Stay Cool: When you would mark Stress, you may spend any amount of Adrenaline to mark one less per 2 Adrenaline spent.
Ready: When you gain the spotlight with less than 2 Adrenaline, set your Adrenaline to 2.
Specialization Features Quick Reflexes: When you are targeted by an attack, spend any amount of Adrenaline to gain an equal bonus to your Evasion against that attack. If that causes the attack to fail, clear a Shield Slot.
Stoked: When you gain the spotlight with less than 3 Adrenaline, set your Adrenaline to 3.
Mastery Features Tactical Reposition: Once per long rest, if you would mark any amount of Hit Points, you can mark that much Stress instead and immediately move a Close range.
Pumped Up: When you gain the spotlight with less than 4 Adrenaline, set your Adrenaline to 4. Your Adrenaline die becomes a d10.
RO6BoGdtB6amQG00.Name: Shock Trooper

2gtbKXBNkMdGHLW7.Description:
Geth are a synthetic hivemind, originally created by the Quarians. Many Geth live in server nodes, but some may choose to control a bipedal mobile platform, consisting of an array of synthetic muscle netting, armor plating, and a single flashlight as their 'head'. These Geth can still connect to other Geth programs, although it will never be as efficient as living directly in the network.
> Geth are not welcome in Citadel space, because of their history with driving the Quarians from their homeworld (in self-defense).
- Synthetic: You do not need to eat, breathe, or sleep. You are immune to poison and organic diseases.
- Neural Network: Make an Instinct roll (12) to connect to the Geth network. On a success, you can communicate across any distance with other Geth.
2gtbKXBNkMdGHLW7.Name: Geth

EOOJXzmCdSLHXjED.Name: Quarian

fHydPZekYQwstGbT.Description:
Hanar are tall, jellyfish-like beings from the ocean planet Kahje. They generally live in water but have the ability to walk and breathe in air with the support of mass effect fields. Their skin is often pink, which shifts color as they speak through bioluminescence. Thankfully, modern translator units are able to convert this into more commonly understood audible language for other species.
> Hanar are known for their intense politeness and use different names depending on who they are speaking with. Learning a Hanar's Soul Name is a great honor.
- Amphibious: You can breathe and move naturally underwater.
- Tentacular: You can swap weapons without marking Stress. Additionally, treat the range of your Melee weapons, abilities, and spells as Very Close.
fHydPZekYQwstGbT.Name: Hanar

FofG5Ehh0tyPtqqR.Description:
Humanoids native to the world of Khar'shan, the Batarians have isolated themselves after several incidents where the strict adherence of their caste-like system caused conflict with other Citadel species. They have pointed ears, several bony protrusions on their face that surround their distinctive set of four eyes. Their skin varies from green/yellow to brownish red, with occasional facial patterns. They do not trust easily and often come across as hostile.
> The Batarian Hegemony closed their embassy on the Citadel and chose to isolate themselves from the rest of the galaxy. The Terminus Systems are infested with batarian pirate gangs and slaving rings, fueling the stereotype of the batarian thug.
- Sturdy: When you have 1 Hit Point remaining, attacks against you have Disadvantage.
- Tormented: While you have 0 or 1 Hope, you may add an Experience to your roll without spending Hope.
FofG5Ehh0tyPtqqR.Name: Batarian

MwQ5VnyFpt13xSas.Description:
Known for their unique biology and aggressive behavior, the vorcha of Heshtok are the shortest-lived sapient species currently known, with an average lifespan of only 20 years. The Vorcha's cell structure mutates to respond to threats, giving them incredible regeneration and recovery from diseases. Yet, this rapid mutation is also responsible for their quickened lifespan. Many Vorcha have sharp teeth, but most of their other features adapt to the environment.
> Vorcha are often found among the galaxy's darker and more dangerous locations. The rest of galactic civilization regards them as pests and scavengers.
- Mutagenic Response: Once per rest when you mark HP, you can go into Overdrive. During Overdrive, you gain resistance to the most recent damage type taken, but you also become Vulnerable. Overdrive ends when you take Severe damage or the scene ends.
- Rapid Regeneration: You may choose to take the long rest version of 'Tend to Wounds' during a short rest, or use the short rest version as an additional downtime action.
MwQ5VnyFpt13xSas.Name: Vorcha

PXM25OAcDXt2U44t.Description:
Salarians are amphibians, native to the planet Sur'Kesh. Their extremely quick metabolism lets them live, think and act at twice the speed of other races. Though, this also means they rarely live past 40 human years. They have slender, tall bodies with large oval-shaped eyes and cartilagous horns.
> Salarians were close behind the Asari in their discovery of the Citadel. They play a vital role in intelligence and research divisions.
- Rapid Analysis: You may mark a Stress to gain Advantage on a Reaction Roll.
- Hyperactive: Gain a permanent +1 bonus to your Evasion at character creation.
{{
PXM25OAcDXt2U44t.Name: Salarian

QMcvdz4ftuSbxrD2.Description:
Turians, originating from Palaven, are humanoids known for their iron discipline, which is matched only by their metallic skin plating. Their faces have mandibles that they use for expression and eyes are sunken in their vaguely dragon-like features. They stand slightly taller than humans and have a similar lifespan.
> Turians are the third species on the Citadel Council. Their fleet is by far the largest of any species and their military is the core that their society is build around.
- Exoskeleton: When you would mark 2 or more Hit Points, you can mark a Stress to mark 1 fewer HP.
- Apex Predator: When you deal at least Major damage to a creature that already has Hit Points marked, they must also mark a Stress.
QMcvdz4ftuSbxrD2.Name: Turian

RDQecjwAWY8x4iTo.Name: Krogan

Tt3IqSBvQhlIQNZv.Description:
Asari are humanoids originating from Thessia, capable of living well over 1000 years. They are mono-gendered - neither male nor female - though they are predominantly considered female-presenting. The hue of their skin is blue to violet, betraying a natural affinity with biotics. They do not develop hair, but they possess semi-flexible scalp-crests that grow into shape. They are capable of short psychic connections with other beings to share thoughts and experiences.
> The Asari are the first race to discover the Citadel. They were instrumental in founding the Council. Naturally gravitating towards cooperation with other species, they have been at the heart of galactic society ever since.
- Natural Biotic: Asari can use Biotic Amps. Additionally, when you make an Instinct Roll, Spend 2 Hope to reroll the Hope die.
- Mind Meld: As an additional downtime action, you can make a mental connection with one ally. You may exchange any amount of Hope and Stress between each other.
Tt3IqSBvQhlIQNZv.Name: Asari

TwbdDhPPDpXDDDTV.Description:
Humans, originally from Earth, have dexterous hands, rounded ears, and can live up to 140 years with proper care. They are remarkably strong-willed and ambitious as they persevere to face the galaxy's unknown. This gives them an advantage in adapting to many situations.
> Humans are relative newcomers in galactic civilization. Some older Humans may still remember a time from before they discovered the Mass Relays. Some inhabitants of the galaxy feel threatened by their quick rise to power.
- Galactic Ambition: Gain an additional Stress slot at character creation.
- Persevere: When you roll with Fear, you may mark 2 Stress to turn it into a roll with Hope.
TwbdDhPPDpXDDDTV.Name: Human

XKpmgdCF38GviCSc.Description:
Elcor are massive four-legged beings from the high-gravity planet of Dekuuna. Because they use all of their limbs for stability, they mount weapons on their backs for combat situations. Their emotions are usually extremely subtle, using pheromones or very slight adjustments in body language when talking with other Elcor. When dealing with other races, they rely on describing their intonations or other methods of conveying their feelings about a situation.
- Walking Tank You can Trample enemies as an improvised weapon - Strength, d8+d6 physical, Melee
- Stoic Speech Once per rest, when you make a Presence Roll you may use another trait in place of your Presence trait.
XKpmgdCF38GviCSc.Name: Elcor

YcSIBUJgxRMjodb3.Description:
Native to the arid desert world Rakhana, the reptile-like Drell are rarely found in the Milky Way. The sparse resources on their homeworld led to the emergence of perfect eidetic memory, to remember oases as they traveled nomadically. As resources dwindled further, the Hanar took steps to evacuate as many Drell as possible, but many still suffered the harsh reality of extinction.
> Drell are not well accustomed to the humidity of the Hanar homeworld, leading many of the rescued Drell to develop a disease called 'Kepral Syndrome'. As of yet, no cure has been discovered, leading many Drell to turn towards
- Eidetic Memory: You can accurately recall any memory. You have Advantage on Knowledge and Instinct Rolls that involve an enemy or location already encountered.
- Survival Sense: Once per rest, mark a Stress to force an adversary to reroll an attack against you or an ally within Very Close range.
YcSIBUJgxRMjodb3.Name: Drell

Ymg7rzBECaFqAdEt.Description:
Volus are small humanoids native to the planet Irune, which possesses a high-pressure greenhouse atmosphere and supports a ammonia-based biochemistry. This is vastly different from the other ancestries which are primarily carbon-oxygen based. As a result, the volus must wear strong pressure suits and breathers when dealing with other species.
> Because they are not physically adept compared to most species, volus mostly make their influence felt through trade and commerce, and they have a long history on the Citadel. However, they have never been invited to join the Council, which is a sore point for many volus individuals.
- Shell: You have a bonus to your damage thresholds equal to your proficiency.
- Pressurized: Once per rest, you can release high-pressure ammonia from your suit to create a temporary toxic cloud in Very Close range that obscures vision.
Ymg7rzBECaFqAdEt.Name: Volus

bwL3DWneBmfE0VAg.Description:
Your home is among the stars, without gravity holding you down or atmosphere bringing you comfort. The sound of recirculating air and the sight of stars through metal are all you know. You may have lived on a mining station, a cargo ship, or among the Migrant Fleet.
- Zero-G Adaptation: You have Advantage on Agility rolls to move in zero-gravity and can easily orient yourself in chaotic situations.
bwL3DWneBmfE0VAg.Name: Spaceborne

DgL6jTZuFTqCrSpb.Description: You may not have necessarily grown up near a large body of water, but you have always been around spaceports and freighter docks. You may have helped with resupplying or maintaining ships that arrived, guiding the crew on their visit, or entertained travelers with a local tradition.
DgL6jTZuFTqCrSpb.Name: Seaborne

EUAY49bTq8KVkTDb.Description: You grew up in high galactic society in a luxurious lifestyle. You may be close to the embassies on the Citadel Presidium, living large in the luxury penthouses dotting the immense skyscrapers near the Silversun Strip, or pursuing lucrative business opportunities on planets like Illium or Bekenstein.
EUAY49bTq8KVkTDb.Name: Highborne

I6bE3A5EiYb3Rsjh.Description: You were part of a strict organization for a part of your life. You could be part of a military organization, but also a security force like C-Sec or Elanus Risk Control Services, working a bureaucratic job at the Citadel Tower, or living in religious centers like one of numerous Hanar temples devoted to the Enkindlers.
I6bE3A5EiYb3Rsjh.Name: Orderborne

IUpdmYPSiqy35ZT9.Description:
You grew up on a world at the edge of known space, where civilization is a fragile outpost and under constant threat of nature, predators, or criminals. You've learned to make the most of any situation despite hardship and that you need to pull your weight in order to survive.
- Frontier Spirit: Once per rest, if you have less than 2 Hope, you may mark a Stress to gain 2 Hope.
IUpdmYPSiqy35ZT9.Name: Frontierborne

nReGk984z7t5JqNI.Description: Your body was forged by the crushing gravity and extreme atmospheric conditions of planets like Tuchanka or Dekuuna, or maybe you became a captive of criminals, that forced you to work in an illegal mining operation.
nReGk984z7t5JqNI.Name: Ridgeborne

NVohPO6jBpY45hT5.Description:
You spent most of your time near the first footsteps of your ancestors, in the core systems of your ancestry. You are particularly well aware of where you came from and how far you've come since as a people.
- Historic Purpose: During character creation, you may choose an Experience that best reflects your time in the core worlds and describe what you learned from it. Give it a permanent +1 bonus.
NVohPO6jBpY45hT5.Name: Coreborne

TmoFRAbZ6xvzDW8F.Description:
You grew up in the lawless territories outside Citadel-protected space - the Terminus Systems. Growing up here, you quickly learned that kindness and trust are a form of weakness to exploit. You might have been a part of mercenary groups on Omega, part of a pirate squad, or a slaver by trade. Alternatively, you might have been subjected to the brutal reign these groups enforced upon you, or you have been fighting tirelessly, often forced to forsake your own principles.
- Drastic Measure: Once per session, when you or an ally you Helped roll with Hope, you can turn it into a roll with Fear to gain +1 on all action and reaction rolls for the remainder of the session.
{{FrameHeader,wide
### Mass Heart
:
TmoFRAbZ6xvzDW8F.Name: Grimborne

UIS6DwsjWPIhHP7A.Description:
You never really felt like settling down in one particular place and wandered between planets, ships and space stations, always traveling where your interest or the circumstances would take you.
### New Communities
UIS6DwsjWPIhHP7A.Name: Wanderborne

x6rcQLljcMuyLIkz.Description: You grew up in centers of research excellence, scientific academies, or digital libraries, where knowledge is the most precious resource. For example you might hail from the prestigious Grissom Academy, or you may have researched relics from a long lost civilization.
x6rcQLljcMuyLIkz.Name: Loreborne

0C7Fi5rHsfRBjLZL.Description: Hot: This weapon cuts through solid material; can attack targets in a line.
0C7Fi5rHsfRBjLZL.Name: Geth Javelin

1n2uZ6iY0yDkgfMY.Description:
### Tier 4 Bruiser
A gargantuan piloted mech suit equipped with a mass accelerator cannon and heavy missile launchers.
Motives & Tactics: Crush cover, grab, endure
#### Features
Missile Barrage - Action: Spend 1 Fear. The Atlas fires a volley at up to three different targets within Far range. Each target must make a Reaction Roll or take 4d8 ene damage and became Vulnerable.
Hydraulic Crush - Action: Make an attack in melee range. If the attack is successful, the target is Restrained.
Hardened Ceramic Plating - Reaction: When the Atlas enters the scene, add 10 Shield tokens.
Gargantuan - Reaction: When the Atlas takes damage, you can Spend a Fear and remove a Shield token to reduce the damage by 15.
Siege Engine - Passive: The Atlas ignores all cover that is not "Heavy" (like a reinforced bunker). It destroys smaller cover (crates, thin walls) simply by moving through or shooting at it.
}}
{{adversary,
1n2uZ6iY0yDkgfMY.Name: Atlas Mech

4DouX60rR2pdRs75.Description:
### Tier 1 Horde (1/HP)
Synthetic-organic "zombies" created from the bodies of organic beings.
Motives & Tactics: Grapple, overwhelm, chase
#### Features
Horde (1d6+1) - Passive: When the Husks have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d6+1 physical damage instead.
Terrifying Hiss (Passive): When the Husks make a successful attack, the target must mark a Stress.
}}
{{adversary,
4DouX60rR2pdRs75.Name: Husks (Reaper)

5yaay4J1X27sVWuv.Description:
### Tier 1 Skulk
A quadruple-legged robotic hound designed for pursuit and takedowns, equipped with a taser bite.
Motives & Tactics: Rush from cover, stun target
#### Features
Ambush Predator - Passive: The Fenris Mech deals direct damage while they’re Hidden.
Neural Shock - Reaction: When the Fenris Mech deals damage with its Taser Bite, the GM may spend 1 Fear to inflict the Shocked condition. Shocked targets have disadvantage on their next action roll, then clear the condition.
High-Speed Servos - Action: The Fenris Mech can move up to Far range as a free movement and immediately take the Hide action.
}}
{{adversary,
5yaay4J1X27sVWuv.Name: Fenris Mech

5YYU24UwQCG59a6d.Description:
### Tier 4 Solo
A floating, horrific amalgamation of husks with a particle beam and an impenetrable barrier.
Motives & Tactics: Tank, clearing area
#### Features
Relentless (2): The Praetorian can be spotlighted up to 2 times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.
Death Beam - Action: The Praetorian maintains its beam on a single target. With a successful attack, if the Praetorian uses the next attack on the same target, it deals an additional 2d12 damage.
Biotic Ground Slam - Action: Spend 2 Fear. The Praetorian crashes into the ground. All targets within Close range take 4d10 phy damage and are pushed to Close range. The Praetorian restores 1 Stress Points per each target.
Ominous Float - Passive: The Praetorian is always hovering. It ignores difficult terrain and can overcome obstacles on the ground.
}}
{{wide
5YYU24UwQCG59a6d.Name: Collector Praetorian

9KeNtfHiASkkRQ78.Description:
### Tier 1 Standard
The Blood Pack mercenaries o0ften employ Vorcha for their resilience and aggression. This variant focuses on area denial and punishing stationary players.
Motives & Tactics: Flush out cover, regenerate, burn
A fragile, bipedal security robot programmed for mass deployment. They march forward firing indiscriminately.
#### Features
Pyro-Shielding - Passive: The Vorcha takes half damage from fire-based attacks.
Vent Heat - Action: Mark a Stress to unleash a burst of flame in a cone. All targets within Very Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 1d6 ene damage and they must mark 1 Shield Slot without gaining its benefits due to thermal overload.
Resilient Biology - Reaction: When the Vorcha marks HP, you can spend a Fear to immediately clear 1 Stress.
}}
{{adversary,
9KeNtfHiASkkRQ78.Name: Blood Pack Vorcha (Flamer)

BNoYcCnzpRd9zRvy.Description:
### Tier 1 Standard
Disciplined and well-equipped mercenaries, known for their blue armor and use of illegal technology.
Motives & Tactics: Mercenary Tactics +2
#### Features
Concussive Shot - Action: Mark 1 Stress to fire a concussive shot at a target within close range. If the attack is successful, the target is knocked back to Close range and, if it hits a solid surface, becomes Vulnerable.
Shield Disruption - Passive: The Trooper uses disruptor ammunition. If the Trooper's attack hits a target that still has active shield slots, it deals +2 damage.
Tactical Retreat - Reaction: When the Trooper takes damage, it can immediately move to a cover area within Close range.
}}
{{adversary,
BNoYcCnzpRd9zRvy.Name: Blue Suns Trooper

Cyh54mtidp8ETSkf.Description:
### Tier 1 Bruiser
A scaled, predatory beast with a massive jaw, often used as an attack dog by Krogans or pirates.
Motives & Tactics: Charge, endure pain, feed
#### Features
Leaping Charge - Action: The Varren moves to a target within Close range. The target must make a Strength Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, they take 4 phy damage and became Restrained.
Ignore the Pain - Reaction: When the Varren marks HP, it can spend 1 Stress to reduce the damage by 1.
Pack Frenzy - Passive: The Varren deals +2 damage if another Varren or ally is within Very Close range of its target.
}}
{{adversary,
Cyh54mtidp8ETSkf.Name: Varren

EeKQ8pcziSgIxc3d.Description:
### Tier 3 Ranged
A bloated, heavy-artillery monstrosity created from a corrupted Rachni, carrying twin cannons.
Motives & Tactics: Fire support, suppress
#### Features
Artillery Bombardment - Action: The Ravager fires into the air. Choose a location within Far range. All targets within Close range of that spot must make an Agility Reaction Roll or take 3d12 ene damage and become Vulnerable.
Swarmer Spawning - Reaction: When the Ravager marks HP from a physical attack, the GM may spend 1 Fear to spawn a number of Small Swarm (Tier 1 Minion, 1 HP, Melee 0, Dmg 1) equal to the HP marked, at the Ravager's feet.
Acid Rupture - Passive: When the Ravager is defeated or its Major Threshold is crossed, it leaves a pool of acid (Close range). Any creature entering or starting their turn in the pool takes 6 ene damage.
}}
{{adversary,
EeKQ8pcziSgIxc3d.Name: Reaper Ravager

eEp1miWNx2S1AzlW.Description:
### Tier 2 Skulk
A disciplined biotic operative who strikes from the shadows.
Motives & Tactics: Isolate targets, disrupt formations, vanish
#### Features
Tactical Cloak - Passive: The Huntress deals direct damage while they’re Hidden.
Warp Field - Action: Mark a Stress to target an enemy within Far range. Make an attack roll. On a success, the target takes standard damage and becomes Vulnerable. Until the end of the scene (or until they clear the condition), the target cannot recover Shield Slots.
Biotic Blink - Reaction: When the Huntress is targeted by an attack, spend a Fear to teleport to a location within Close range, potentially causing the attack to miss. The attack made against the Huntress has Disadvantage.
}}
{{adversary,
eEp1miWNx2S1AzlW.Name: Asari Huntress

g6WPLwccJd2ayKv2.Description:
### Tier 3 Horde
A buzzing cloud of mechanical insects that seeks to sting and paralyze targets for harvest.
Motives & Tactics: Paralyze, overwhelm
#### Features
Collector Paralysis - Passive: Any target hit by the Seeker Swarm must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they gain the Restrained condition. While Restrained this way, they cannot gain or spend Hope.
Ominous Buzzing - Action: The swarm fills a zone (Close range). All targets in the zone must make an Instinct Roll (Difficulty 17). On a failure, they mark 2 Stress and are Vulnerable.
Flight - Passive: The Swarm can move over obstacles and ignore difficult terrain. It has Advantage on Agility-based rolls.
}}
{{adversary,
g6WPLwccJd2ayKv2.Name: Seeker Swarm (Collector)

GatMsVSnG2omRfbI.Description:
### Tier 2 Support
A tactical specialist wearing distinct yellow and white armor, deploying turrets to control the battlefield.
Motives & Tactics: Deploy tech, strip defenses, flush out targets
#### Features
Incinerate -Action: Mark a Stress to fire a superheated plasma blast at a target within Close range. On a success, deal 2d6+4 ene damage. If the target has no active Shield Slots, they are Vulnerable.
Deploy Turret - Action: Spend a Fear. The Engineer places a Sentry Turret (Tier 1 Minion, stationary, Ranged attack +3, Dmg 4) in a zone within Close range. The Turret immediately takes the spotlight.
Overload - Reaction: When a PC within Far range uses the "Take Cover" action or a "Success with Hope" to recover Shield Slots, the Engineer can mark a Stress to force the target to make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, the PC recovers 0 Shield Slots and takes 1d6 ene damage.
}}
{{adversary,
GatMsVSnG2omRfbI.Name: Cerberus Engineer

ICJv5iS26jfwM968.Description:
### Tier 1 Minion
Geth drones used to flush out the enemy
Motives & Tactics: Acquire, distract , self-destruction
#### Features
Minion (4) - Passive: The Attack Drone is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC dealts to the Attack Drone, defeat an additional Minion whitin range the attack would succeed against.
Self-Destruct Protocol - Reaction: When destroyed, the Attack Drone deals 1 damage to anyone within Melee range.
Group Attack - Action: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Attack Drone within Close range of them. Those Minions make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage each. Combine this damage.
}}
{{adversary,
ICJv5iS26jfwM968.Name: Geth Attack Drone

kSDaPuEu6lhT2stO.Description:
### Tier 3 Social/Special
A genetic variant of the Asari who dominates victims psychically and burns their nervous system.
Motives & Tactics: Seduce, dominate, terrorize
#### Features
Dominate Mind - Action: The Ardat-Yakshi locks eyes with a target. The target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they must mark 3 Stress, they gain immediately the Spotlight, and the GM chooses their next action (must be an attack against an ally or a move away from the objective).
Embrace Eternity - Action: Make an attack against a Restrained or Vulnerable target at Melee range. If successful, deal 5d10 ene damage. The Ardat-Yakshi clears 2 HP and 2 Stress as she drains the target’s life force.
Aura of Terror - Passive: Any target starting their action within Close range of the Ardat-Yakshi must mark 1 Stress or have Disadvantage on all rolls.
}}
{{adversary,
kSDaPuEu6lhT2stO.Name: Ardat-Yakshi

KZdxSwmLLoF3P2Uj.Description:
### Tier 2 Minion
A mindlessly aggressive cybernetic zombie. They swarm in terrifying numbers.
Motives & Tactics: Swarm, grab and bite
#### Features
Minion (10): For every 6 points of damage dealt, defeat an additional Husk in the group.
Static Pulse - Reaction: When a Husk is defeated in Melee, it releases an electrical burst. The attacker must make an Instinct Reaction Roll or mark 1 Stress.
Weight of Numbers - Passive: If a PC is within Melee range of 3 or more Husks, they are Restrained and cannot move until they succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll.
}}
{{adversary,
KZdxSwmLLoF3P2Uj.Name: Advanced Husk

m8GIwtNZofwIVuKQ.Description:
### Tier 1 Ranged
These soldiers function with determined precision and practiced teamwork
Motives & Tactics: Acquire, cover, eliminate
#### Features
Tactical Cover - Reaction: Mark 1 Stress to gain +2 Difficulty against a ranged attack if near cover.
Smoke Grenade - Action: Creates a Very Close area that grants Disadvantage to all ranged attack rolls made into or out of the area.
}}
{{adversary,
m8GIwtNZofwIVuKQ.Name: Cerberus Assault Trooper

mqo6oBzCTdhKGs4Z.Description:
### Tier 2 Skulk
An Asari mercenary clad in yellow armor, crackling with biotic energy.
Motives & Tactics: Charge in, flank, biotic detonation
#### Features
Biotic Charge - Action: Mark a Stress. The Vanguard dissolves into blue energy and reappears within Melee range of a target within Far range, immediately making a standard attack. If the attack hits, the target is knocked back to Close range and became Vulnerable.
Warp Ammo - Passive: The Vanguard deals +1d8 damage against targets who have at least 1 active Shield Slot.
Biotic Barrier Initialization - Reaction: When Vangard enters the battlefield, add 3 Biotic Barrier tokens.

## Suggested GM Moves
In this section you will find suggestions for moves that can be activated by spending 1 or more Fear points to drastically change the course of a scene or put the characters in serious difficulty.
}}
#### Combat and Technology
These moves focus on the high-tech nature of the Mass Effect universe, where hardware failure or tactical precision can be as deadly as a bullet.
- 1 Fear: A PC's weapon or omni-tool malfunctions. The PC cannot use that specific equipment or a related tech ability until they spend an Action (or mark 1 Stress) to manual-reset the system.
- 1 Fear: Instead of dealing physical damage, inflict 2 Stress on a PC, representing a kinetic barrier overload or a debilitating neural shock.
- 1 Fear: A group of enemies coordinates their fire. Until the PC moves to a different Range or finds new cover, they take Disadvantage on all Attack Rolls.
- 1 Fear: An enemy hacker or an automated turret identifies a weakness in a PC’s armor. The next time that PC marks Shield to reduce damage, they must mark two Shield Slots instead of one.
- 2 Fear: Spend this when a PC is out of cover or separated from the group. An elite unit (like an Atlas Mech or a Geth Hunter) moves instantly to Melee or Very Close range and immediately makes an Attack Roll with Advantage.
:
#### Environment & Galactic Tension
These moves leverage the vacuum of space, hostile alien worlds, and the complex political landscape of the Citadel and beyond.
- 1 Fear: A localized jammer or solar flare cuts the feed to the ship or HQ. PCs lose access to external intel or support (like orbital scans or remote hacking) for the remainder of the scene.
- 2 Fear: A hull breach or a toxic leak occurs. The GM starts a 4-segment Countdown Clock. When it fills, the area becomes uninhabitable; PCs must either seal the breach or start marking 1 HP directly every time they take an action without a pressurized suit.
- 1 Fear: A stray shot hits a volatile fuel cell or a power conduit. The GM creates a new Hazard on the field (e.g., Dangerous terrain that deals d8 Magic damage to anyone ending their turn nearby).
- 1 Fear: Use this in social or high-security situations. A Citadel official or a local warlord demands a "security tax" or a permit. The PCs must either spend a resource (like a specific loot item or local currency) or take Disadvantage on their next Social roll to convince or bypass them.
- 2 Fear: The presence of Reaper technology weighs on the mind. Every PC in the area must immediately mark 1 Stress. Additionally, for the rest of the scene, any roll that results in Fear causes the PC to see a hallucination of a loved one or a traumatic memory, momentarily distracting them.
#### Social Intrigue & NPC Influence
These moves represent the complex web of loyalties, diplomatic friction, and personal costs involved in galactic politics.
- 1 Fear: An NPC reveals they know something compromising about a PC’s past or their current mission. The PC must either mark 1 Stress to remain stoic or give in to a minor demand to keep the NPC quiet.
- 1 Fear: After a PC makes a social roll (even a success), an NPC subtly undermines their authority in front of an audience. The next social roll made by any PC in this scene takes Disadvantage.
- 1 Fear: An ally or contact provides help, but they demand an immediate and inconvenient favor in return. If the PCs refuse, they lose a Bond or a point of Reputation with that faction.
- 1 Fear: A PC inadvertently offends a sensitive alien custom (e.g., Turian hierarchy or Krogan rites). The GM creates a Countdown Clock (3-segments) called "Diplomatic Fallout." If it fills before the PCs apologize or fix it, the NPC becomes Hostile.
- 2 Fear: A PC antagonist appears in a moment of great impact, complicating the scene with emotional hooks from the past.

0.Name: Mass Heart GM Moves

0.Content:
## Campaign Frame Mechanics
These are mechanics specific to this campaign frame.
}}
### Magic Damage and Spells
In a Mass Effect campaign, Magic damage is called Energy damage. Energy damage can come from different sources - Biotic, Tech, or non-projectile weapons. Some abilities may care about the type of source that causes damage.
Spells and Spellcast Rolls are similarly divided into Biotic and Tech, depending on the character class.
:
### Armor
In this campaign frame, you still have access to the regular sets of armor, but this armor is equipped with personal shield generators. This makes them behave slightly different.
:
#### Shield Slots
The Armor Slots on your character sheet are used as Shield Slots which reflect the strength of your Kinetic Barrier. You have a number of Shield Slots equal to half your Armor Score, rounded down. This also means that any effects that change your Armor Score have a halved effect on your Shield Slots.
Unlike standard armor that requires repairs, any marked Shield Slots are automatically cleared during downtime. Downtime can be a rest, but also any other sufficiently long period of low tension.
:
#### Taking Cover
It's also possible to replenish Shield Slots during tense situations. Several classes have abilities or features that restore Shield Slots for themselves or allies. Additionally, every character can 'Take Cover' to restore Shield Slots.
- Take Cover Make an Action Roll to take cover and become Hidden. If you roll with Hope, you may immediately clear 1 Shield Slot instead of gaining Hope.
> The trait used for 'Take Cover' depends on the narrative; it does not need to be Finesse because of 'hiding'. Work together to describe the cover to make it fit in the story.
:
#### Overshield
Some abilities apply "Overshield". Overshield applies temporary Shield Slots on top of your regular Shield Slots, with some special rules.
- Overshield can not be recharged. Once you mark the Shield Slot, it is depleted until a new source of Overshield is applied.
- Overshield does not stack. When you have Overshield 1 and gain Overshield 2 from another source, the new source overrides the old one. You'll have 2 temporary Shield Slots, not 3.
- Any remaining Overshield clears when you take a rest.
#### New Downtime Moves
With automatic Shield regeneration, the "Repair Armor" downtime move no longer exists. These alternative downtime moves are available instead.
##### Short Rest
- Overcharge: Gain Overshield 1.
- Talk to the Team: Choose a teammate. Until your next rest, you can both use a d20 as a Hope die for the next Tag Team Roll you make together.
:
##### Long Rest
- Recalibrate: Gain a permanent Shield Slot until your next long rest.
- Talk to the Crew: Speak to your group about your adventure and the choices you made. Describe the scene, then gain 1 Morality point*.
> \* Morality Points are described on the next page!
:
### Domains
#### Sage
When using this frame, there are likely few places that have not been touched by civilization at some point. This can make abilities that rely on your natural connection with the world narratively unsatisfying.
We've chosen to reflavor the Sage domain to fit a wider interpretation of 'connection'. Think about how you connect to your surroundings as a whole. Feel the silent hum of the Element Zero that is used to power the things around you. Sage spells are deeply connected with biotics, gravity, and mass-altering effects. Use this information to guide your descriptions for any Sage-related abilities and spells.
:
#### Valor
Armorer (Level 5) does not work, as there is no downtime move to repair armor. Instead, when you 'Overcharge' you can apply the effect to one other ally.
As mentioned in the section about Kinetic Barriers, any gains to Armor Score are converted into Shield Slots with the conversion rate of 2 Armor Score > 1 Shield Slot. You can instead choose to change the effect into granting Overshield, if this causes confusion.

0.Name: Mass Heart Mechanics

0.Content:
## Overview
If your group decides to play this campaign, give your players the following information before character creation.
}}
The Milky Way galaxy is a vast, interconnected web of ancient secrets and burgeoning civilizations, all linked by the enigmatic Mass Relays. In this campaign frame, players step into a world where the discovery of Prothean technology has catapulted humanity into a galactic community governed by the Citadel Council. It is a setting of high-stakes diplomacy, breathtaking exploration, and the looming shadow of an ancient threat. As players traverse the stars, they will find themselves at the heart of a cosmic drama where every choice resonates through the vacuum of space, balanced between the cold logic of survival and the burning hope of unity.
The galactic landscape is defined by its diverse inhabitants and the fragile peace maintained by the Council. From the disciplined hierarchy of the Turian Hierarchy to the brilliant, fast-paced innovation of the Salarian Union, and the centuries-long wisdom of the Asari Republics, each culture brings its own philosophy to the table. Players can choose to embody these iconic races or others, like the resilient Krogan or the technologically nomadic Quarians. In Mass Heart, these ancestries are more than just stats; they are the lenses through which characters view a galaxy that is often hostile, yet filled with potential allies.
The world of Mass Heart is a duality of gleaming utopias and gritty underworlds. The Citadel stands as a beacon of civilization, a massive space station where laws are debated and the fate of trillions is decided. Contrast this with Omega, a lawless station in the Terminus Systems where mercenaries, smugglers, and outcasts thrive in the neon-lit shadows. Players will find themselves jumping from corporate laboratories on frozen moons to ancient ruins on lush, uncharted worlds, utilizing their Omni-tools and biotics to overcome obstacles that range from political scandals to localized extinction events.
Factions pull the strings of this galaxy, creating a complex web of loyalty and betrayal. The Systems Alliance represents humanity’s ambition and drive, while the shadowy organization known as Cerberus operates on the fringes, championing human supremacy at any cost. Meanwhile, the Geth—a collective AI consciousness—and the mysterious Collectors remind the galaxy that not all threats come from within the Council’s borders. Navigating these organizations requires more than just firepower; it requires the social finesse and tactical brilliance that Daggerheart’s "Spotlight" and "Hope/Fear" mechanics excel at portraying.
The gameplay experience in Mass Heart merges tactical combat with cinematic narrative. Combat is fast and lethal, emphasizing the use of cover, tech overrides, and devastating biotic fields.
Space travel is the lifeblood of the campaign. The players’ ship is not just a vehicle, but a home and a base of operation, a "silent character" that grows with the crew. Whether you are hunting a rogue Spectre across the Attican Traverse or investigating a distress signal from a derelict freighter, the ship provides the sanctuary needed for Downtime moves. Here, characters bond, upgrade their gear, and prepare for the inevitable moment when diplomacy fails and the guns must be drawn.
As the campaign progresses, the true nature of the cosmic cycle begins to reveal itself through fragmented whispers and terrifying anomalies. Ancient, sentient horrors of unimaginable power wait in the freezing dark space between galaxies, watching and preparing for their periodic harvest of all organic life. This existential threat provides a silent, ticking clock for the narrative, as the players uncover evidence of a doom that has claimed countless civilizations before their own. These revelations force characters to attempt the impossible: uniting warring factions against a shadow they can barely comprehend. The stakes are nothing less than the survival of every sentient being in the galaxy, turning every mission into a desperate, crucial step toward salvation or an inevitable, silent extinction.
Commanders, the galaxy is at a crossroads. The stars are beautiful, but they are also cold and indifferent to the struggles of those who dwell among them. It takes a special kind of hero to bridge the gap between species, to stand against the tide of darkness, and to prove that the heart of a team is stronger than the cold steel of a machine. Assemble your crew, calibrate your weapons, and prepare to make your mark on the stars.
The fate of the Milky Way is in your hands, will you be the light that guides it home?

0.Content:
## Morality and Reputation
The Morality and Reputation systems define a character's method of operation and how they are perceived by the galaxy. They have two opposing ideologies. Paragons are inspiring leaders who strive to uphold the law and Renegades are determined pragmatists who put results above all else. Try to avoid treating them as 'Good' vs 'Evil', each ideology has their own strengths and shortcomings.
#### Morality
Each character has a two-sided tracker for Morality. When you gain a Morality point, you shift the tracker in that direction; when you gain Renegade Morality, you lose Paragon Morality, and vice versa.
- Paragon Morality These are earned by acting with diplomacy, altruism, following protocols, and mercy.
- Renegade Morality These are earned through intimidation, brute force, pragmatism, and achieving your goal at all costs.
Morality can be gained in several ways. At the end of a scene, the GM can award Paragon or Renegade Morality to characters depending on the PC's behavior during the scene. Additionally, the new "Talk to the Crew" downtime move lets players earn Morality of their choice.
The maximum Morality you can have towards a particular side is 3. If you gain any more Morality towards that direction, the effects are ignored. If you gain Morality in the opposite direction, your Morality reduces to 2.
#### Reputation
Reputation represents how you are remembered by the galaxy as the word spreads of your heroic deeds.
When a character levels up, they gain a Paragon or a Renegade Reputation Point, depending on the standing of their Morality at that time. If their Morality is Neutral, they may choose which type of Reputation point to get.
Reputation Points are not opposed; you can accumulate both Paragon and Renegade Reputation simultaneously. After you gain a Reputation Point, your Morality is reset to Neutral.
##### Reputation Abilities
When you have enough Paragon or Renegade Reputation, you gain the corresponding ability from the table below.
#### Graceful & Dreadful Reputation
The Grace and Dread domains are not used in the 6 base classes. Instead, these domains are tied to your Reputation as a Paragon (Grace) or Renegade (Dread). When a player chooses the Pick a Domain Card level-up option, they may take a card of their level or lower from additional domains.
- If Paragon Reputation is higher, take a card from the 'Grace' domain.
- If Renegade Reputation is higher, take a card from the 'Dread' domain.
- If Reputation is equal for both, choose from either 'Grace' or 'Dread' domain.
Presence is your Spellcasting Trait for the cards of these domains. The player may still choose to gain a card from their 'normal' domains instead.
> If you don't own the Hope and Fear expansion, you can use the Dread domain from the public playtest, or have Renegades choose from any domain (keep the party composition in mind).
#### Morality Interruptions
Interruptions allow you to break the normal flow of the narrative to perform iconic actions tied to your character's nature. As long as you have at least 2 Paragon or Renegade Reputation, you may use the respective Interruption. If you use an Interruption, you also gain the corresponding Morality.
- Paragon Intervention Once per session, when a character in Close range takes a hostile action, Spend 2 Hope and declare a Paragon Intervention. Make a Presence Reaction Roll against their difficulty. On a success, you de-escalate the situation or convince someone to take your side or follow your lead.
- Renegade Interruption Once per session, when you roll a Failure with Fear, you can Mark a Stress and declare a Renegade Interrupt. Describe how you "burn" the scene. Your roll becomes a Success with Fear, but the GM gains 1 additional Fear to spend on consequences.
:
#### Optional: Moral Experience
During character creation, you may reflect your innate morality by using one of your two experiences as a Moral Experience (e.g., "Alliance Diplomat (Paragon) +2" or "Ruthless Ex-Mercenary (Renegade) +2"). You gain +1 to that experience while the matching Morality has the most points. You gain a -1 malus if the other Morality has more.

## Weapons
This following weapons are available in this campaign. Share these tables with the players during character creation.
}}
### Weapon Scaling
The weapons in these tables have been separated by Tier in order to reflect their rarity and overall availability among merchants. The tier where the weapon appears is generally the earliest moment it can be acquired.
Weapons of lower tiers remain viable as they can be found in higher Tiers when characters reach higher levels. This can be represented by custom modifications, better manufacturers, or overall higher quality.
For each tier increase, the damage of the weapon increases by 3 (2 for Secondary Weapons).
If an effect mentions a "+Tier" in the description of its effect, that effect scales when the weapon is found in higher tiers.
> It's generally not advised to downscale weapons, because their special features can make them extremely powerful on lower levels.
::
#### Optional: Heavy Weapons
There is a special table with Heavy weapons, which are much stronger than most regular weapons. The caveat is that these weapons require a lot of energy to fire, that normal power cells and thermal clips can't provide.
All Heavy weapons are carried like regular weapons. Remember that switching weapons during combat requires marking a Stress. Additionally, each Heavy Weapon has the Ammo feature.
> Ammo X: The weapon can have up to X Ammo tokens on it. When you make an attack with this weapon, remove an Ammo Token. If it has no Ammo, the weapon cannot be used.
Ammo tokens do not replenish during rests. If you decide to use Heavy Weapons, make sure to it clear to everyone at the table whether the weapon is discarded after its Ammo is spent, or if there will be possibilities to replenish Ammo tokens.
If you choose to allow replenishing Ammo, Heavy Weapon Ammo can be made available from merchants or by scavenging. These can be individual Ammo tokens, or packs that restore the entire capacity at once. Loading new Ammo into a weapon during downtime is free, but characters must Mark a Stress to do so during combat.
### Weapon Mechanics
- Amp (Weapon Type) Amplifiers are only available to characters that are already naturally capable of Biotics: Sentinel, Adept, Vanguard, or using the Asari Ancestry.
- Overheat X When you attack using this weapon, add a Heat token to it. If you take an action without this weapon, remove 1 Heat. If it has more than X Heat Tokens, it cannot be used. Mark a Stress to clear all Heat. Heat automatically clear at the end of a scene.
- Sidearm dX When an attack with your primary weapon would miss in range of this weapon, you may add 1dX to the attack roll. If that would succeed against the adversary, deal this weapon's damage. The attack is still considered a Failure.
- Reloading After an attack, roll a d6. On a 1, you must Mark a Stress to un-jam and reload the weapon before you can use it again.
- Quick Mark a Stress to attack another adversary in range.

## The Inciting Incidents
You can use one of the prompts below to start your campaign, or create your own.
}}
#### The Beacon’s Echo (Era: The Eden Prime Legacy)
The galaxy is still reeling from the discovery of humanity, and tensions are high on the Citadel as the newest species struggles to find its place. Your crew has been tasked with a high-priority escort mission to a remote research outpost in the Attican Traverse. Upon arrival, the silence is deafening. The colony’s communications are dead, and as your shuttle breaks the cloud layer, you see the charred remains of a settlement that was thriving only hours ago. A massive, jagged ship of unknown design (resembling a giant, metallic hand) is seen tearing through the upper atmosphere, leaving a wake of distorted gravity behind.
In the center of the ruins, a Prothean Beacon has been unearthed, vibrating with a primal hum that vibrates in the marrow of your bones. It’s emitting a rhythmic pulse that overloads Omni-tools and creates a localized distortion field. As you approach, the mechanical chatter of Geth troopers begins to echo from the surrounding treeline. You are the only ones close enough to secure the artifact before it falls into the hands of the machine-consensus, but the Beacon’s energy is volatile. Every step closer feels like a hammer blow to your mind, threatening to push your resolve to the breaking point.
:::
#### Shadows of the Collector (Era: The Terminus Void)
The Citadel Council insists the "Reaper threat" died with Sovereign, dismissing the warnings as Geth propaganda. But in the lawless Terminus Systems, the truth is far more terrifying: entire colonies are vanishing in a single night. Your crew, operating from a refurbished freighter docked at the neon-soaked Omega nebula, is approached by a shadowy contact with a lead too grim to ignore. They provide coordinates to a "Stilled" colony, a settlement where life hasn't been extinguished by fire, but simply erased.
Walking through the streets of the colony is like walking through a museum of a life interrupted. You find half-eaten meals still warm on tables and vehicles still idling in the streets, but not a single body, human or alien, remains. As the sun sets, a low, buzzing sound fills the air. Thousands of Seekers, tiny, glowing insects of biomechanical origin, begin to swarm from the shadows. You soon realize you aren't just investigating a crime scene; you are the next harvest. Somewhere in the dark, a Collector General is watching through the eyes of its swarm, and you must find a way to escape or become another statistic in the galaxy's growing list of the missing.
#### Pathfinders of the Heleus (Era: The Andromeda Initiative)
After six hundred years of drifting through the dark space between galaxies, you were supposed to wake up to a new beginning. Instead, you awaken to a nightmare. The Ark has been struck by "The Scourge", a sprawling, chaotic cloud of dark energy that has crippled the ship’s systems and left thousands of pioneers trapped in malfunctioning cryo-pods. As part of an emergency Pathfinder team, you are deployed to the surface of Habitat 7, a world that was promised to be a lush paradise but is now a jagged wasteland of floating rocks and perpetual lightning storms.
The mission is simple but desperate: find a way to stabilize the Ark’s power core before the life support fails for the thousands still sleeping. However, you are not alone in this new galaxy. Amidst the ruins of an ancient, self-replicating architecture that defies all known laws of physics, you encounter the Kett, a militaristic alien race that views your arrival not as a diplomatic first contact, but as a biological resource to be exploited. With your supplies running low and your ship falling apart in orbit, you must decide what it means to be a hero in a place where no one knows your name and the very air is trying to kill you.
:::
## Session Zero Questions
Ask your players one or more of these questions or make up
your own.
- When your ship’s signature appears on a station's radar, what is the first thing people think of you?
- Which two members of the crew have served together the longest, and what was the "impossible mission" that cemented your trust?
- Is your ship a cutting-edge Alliance prototype, a refurbished freighter from the Terminus Systems, or a gift from a shadowy benefactor with strings attached?
- In a galaxy of diverse species and ancient grudges, what is one cultural misunderstanding or historical bias that currently creates tension within your crew?
- Does the crew operate within the laws of the Citadel Council, or do you find that getting the job done often requires stepping into the gray areas of galactic law?
- Do we want a story about idealistic heroes trying to save everyone, or a gritty tale of survivors making the cold choices necessary for the greater good?
- Which galactic themes, such as indoctrination, the rights of synthetic life, or genetic manipulation, are we most excited to explore together?
